title: Schedules
lead: Use this skill when the user asks to create, edit, or query Revit schedules. Covers schedule creation (custom, from template, duplicate), column management (add, remove, reorder, rename, hide), row filtering, sorting and grouping (headers, footers, grand totals, itemize), column formatting (alignment, units, totals), appearance (text styles, grid lines, outline, row striping), placing schedules on sheets, and reading schedule data. Also use when the user mentions ViewSchedule, schedule fields, or schedule formatting.
prerequisites: For schedule creation: elements of that category must exist (or schedule will be empty)
For schedule data reads: schedule must already exist
For appearance: text styles should be set up first (see Object Styles Skill)
how_it_flows: Ask Jason to build schedules. He'll start by looking at where you already are.

If you're inside a Revit model, he checks the open document for existing schedules and the elements that would feed each one. If you're in a connected workspace folder, he scans the folder for existing schedules, the client brief, and the project specification.
Before generating anything new, Jason asks for:

Once those are in hand, Jason builds and saves the schedules into your project's `Schedules/` folder. He then runs a cross-check against the model and produces a gap analysis: what's in the schedules that isn't in the model, what's in the model that's missing from the schedules, and which rows have stale or placeholder data.

If you have calculations connected (heat-loss spreadsheet, IESVE export, sizing calc), Jason tells you which schedule rows are out of sync — for example, a radiator whose output in the model doesn't match the calculated load.

settings.description: Choose between `Adelphos templates` or `Upload your own` (.rfa schedule template).
Override which columns appear, their order, and which fields are visible vs hidden-for-filter.
When on, every modelled element pulls from the preferred-manufacturer list. When off, generic families are used.
When on, schedules populate with placeholder values (sizes, models, ratings) so the structure is in place before final selection.

phases.title: Discover Existing Schedules
phases.when_triggers: Before creating schedules, or when user asks "what schedules do we have?", "show me the equipment schedule".

steps.title: LIST ALL SCHEDULES
steps.agent_quote: Model has {N} schedules: - Mechanical Equipment Schedule (24 rows, 6 columns) - Duct Schedule (156 rows, 8 columns) - Space Schedule (89 rows, 10 columns) ...

steps.title: GET SCHEDULE STRUCTURE (if user asks for details)
steps.agent_quote: Equipment schedule structure: Fields: Family, Type, Count, Level, Space, System Type Filters: Level = 'Level 01' Sort: Level (ascending, with headers) → Type (ascending) Appearance: B&W_2.5mm body text, grid lines on

steps.title: GET SCHEDULE DATA (if user asks to see the data)

phases.title: Create a New Schedule
phases.when_triggers: User asks "create an equipment schedule", "make me a duct schedule", "I need a space schedule", or during model setup.

steps.title: USE PRE-CONFIGURED TEMPLATE
steps.agent_quote: Created 'Mechanical Equipment Schedule' with 6 columns.

steps.title: CREATE CUSTOM
steps.agent_quote: Available fields for Mechanical Equipment: Family, Type, Mark, Level, Space, System Type, System Name, Comments, Description, Manufacturer, Model, Count...

steps.title: DUPLICATE EXISTING
steps.user_prompt: copy the equipment schedule and filter it for Level 02

phases.title: Add, Remove, and Reorder Columns
phases.when_triggers: User asks "add a zone column", "remove the comments field", "move level to the first column", "rename the heading".

steps.title: READ CURRENT STRUCTURE
steps.agent_quote: Current columns: Family | Type | Count | Level | Space | System Type

steps.title: ADD COLUMN
steps.agent_quote: Added 'Zone' column.

steps.title: REMOVE COLUMN
steps.agent_quote: Removed 'Comments' column.

steps.title: REORDER
steps.agent_quote: Moved 'Level' to first position.
steps.user_prompt: move Level to the first column

steps.title: RENAME HEADING
steps.agent_quote: Renamed column heading to 'Service'.

steps.title: HIDE COLUMN
steps.agent_quote: Hidden 'Mark' column (still available for filters and sorts).
steps.user_prompt: hide the Mark column but keep it for filtering

steps.title: ADD CALCULATED FIELD
steps.agent_quote: Added 'Count' calculated field.

phases.title: Filter Schedule Rows
phases.when_triggers: User asks "filter to only show LTHW pipes", "show only Level 01 equipment", "remove the filter".

steps.title: READ CURRENT FILTERS
steps.agent_quote: Current filters: none

steps.title: ADD FILTER
steps.agent_quote: Filtered by System Type contains 'LTHW'. {M} rows now visible.
steps.user_prompt: filter to only show LTHW pipes

steps.title: MULTIPLE FILTERS
steps.agent_quote: Added filter: Level = Level 01. Filters are AND — both must match.

steps.title: CLEAR FILTERS
steps.agent_quote: Cleared all filters. {M} rows now showing.

phases.title: Sort and Group Rows
phases.when_triggers: User asks "sort by level", "group by system type", "add totals", "show a count".

steps.title: SORT
steps.agent_quote: Sorted by Level (with group headers) then by Type.
steps.user_prompt: sort by Level then by Type

steps.title: GROUP WITH FOOTERS
steps.agent_quote: Grouped by System Type with header and footer rows.
steps.user_prompt: group by System Type with subtotals

steps.title: GRAND TOTALS
steps.agent_quote: Grand total row added.
steps.user_prompt: show a grand total at the bottom

steps.title: ITEMIZE
steps.agent_quote: Grouped identical rows — showing count per unique combination.
steps.user_prompt: don't list every instance, just show counts

phases.title: Format Columns
phases.when_triggers: User asks "centre the count column", "change the length units", "add totals to the length column", "right-align the numbers".

steps.title: ALIGNMENT
steps.agent_quote: Count column centred.

steps.title: FIELD TOTALS
steps.agent_quote: Length column now shows sum in footer.
steps.user_prompt: show total length at the bottom

steps.title: FORMAT OPTIONS
steps.agent_quote: Length formatted as metres (2 d.p.).
steps.user_prompt: show length in metres with 2 decimal places

phases.title: Set Schedule Appearance
phases.when_triggers: User asks "change the text style", "add grid lines", "turn on stripe rows", "hide the title", "make headers bold".

steps.title: SET APPEARANCE
steps.agent_quote: Updated appearance: title 5mm Bold, headers 3.5mm Bold, body 2.5mm, grid lines on, outline on.

phases.title: Place Schedule on Sheet
phases.when_triggers: User asks "put the equipment schedule on sheet M-00-01", "place all schedules on their sheets".

steps.title: CHECK SPACE
steps.agent_quote: Sheet M-00-01 has 400x250mm free space.

steps.title: PLACE
steps.agent_quote: Placed 'Mechanical Equipment Schedule' on sheet M-00-01.

phases.title: Read Schedule Data
phases.when_triggers: User asks about quantities, counts, or data: "how many AHUs are there?", "what's on Level 02?", "show me the pipe schedule data".

steps.title: ANSWER THE QUESTION
steps.agent_quote: There are 24 mechanical equipment items: - 8 x AHU-01 on Level 01 - 4 x FCU-01 on Level 02 - 12 x VAV Box on Levels 01-03

steps.title: FOR SPECIFIC QUESTIONS
steps.agent_quote: How many ducts on Level 01?
